creator: Alexander Terp
description:
Surfaced during round-2 verification (2026-05-25), test 13a (minor UX).

```rad
fn f(x: str):
    switch x:
        default:
            print("d")
        case "a":
            print("a")
```

`./bin/radd check` reports:
```
L1:1: ERROR Invalid syntax (RAD10001)
L2:5: ERROR Invalid function syntax
```

The errors point at the function definition and the switch header, not at the actual issue: `default:` is the first arm in the switch, before any `case` arms. Swapping to `default` last makes it parse cleanly. So the structural constraint is real (default must come last), but the parser bails with a misleading message far from the actual problem.

**Fix shape:** tree-sitter error recovery or post-parse AST check that detects default-before-case and emits something like:
```
RAD????: 'default' arm must appear last in a switch; reorder it after all 'case' arms.
```

Low priority - users hitting this will eventually figure it out. But the current error sends them looking at their function signature, which is unrelated.
title: Switch with default-arm before case-arms gives misleading 'Invalid function syntax' error
